political.s: FEC: $22K PAC donations; 36% to Republican, 64% to Democratic committees.

execPay.s: CEO total compensation $0K — SEC proxy statement (DEF 14A, 2022). No CEO-to-worker pay ratio disclosed in a parseable form.

wiki.extract: The Gap, Inc., commonly known as Gap Inc., headquartered in San Francisco, California, is an American multinational clothing and accessories retailer. The company operates four primary divisions: the namesake Gap, Banana Republic, Old Navy, and Athleta. Gap Inc. is one of the largest apparel retailers in the United States. It operates 3,569 stores. Zac Posen is the creative director of the Gap.

cases.court: United States Bankruptcy Court, N.D. California

cases.caseName: Carpenter v. Union Gap Village Condo Owner's Association

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy

sampleRecalls.title: Fisher-Price Recalls Tissue Box Toy Sold with SnugaPuppy Activity Centers Due to Choking Hazard
sampleRecalls.description: This recall involves the tissue box toy sold with the Fisher-Price 3-in-1 SnugaPuppy Activity Centers. The activity center's model number (HLV78) is located on the underside of the table, near the Fisher-Price logo. The tissue box toy is white with red decorations and has "tissues" made of a sensory cloth with a black…
hazards: The detachable tissue box toy can come apart, exposing the small support brackets, posing a choking hazard to young children.
injuries: Fisher-Price has received one report of the tissue box toy coming apart and an infant placing the small support bracket in their mouth. No injuries have been reported.
products: Tissue Box Toy sold with Fisher-Price 3-in-1 SnugaPuppy Activity Centers
units: About 15,300 (In addition, about 2,000 were sold in Canada)

sampleRecalls.title: Shower Benches Recalled Due to Fall Hazard; Made by Ivena and Sold Exclusively at Costco (Recall Alert)
sampleRecalls.description: This recall involves Ivena 20" teak shower benches. The benches are made of light brown teak with padding on the feet. The seat and bottom shelf have a row of wood paneling. Item number 1049998 and UPC 8886474018015 are printed on the exterior packaging. The bench has a label that says, "100% wood from well-managed fo…
hazards: The shower bench can collapse during use, posing a fall hazard to the user.
injuries: Consumers have reported 81 incidents of the shower benches collapsing, breaking during use, or falling apart, including four reports of people being injured in falls. The four injuries included a fra…
units: About 70,000 (In addition, about 11,317 were sold in Canada)

sampleRecalls.title: LF Products Recalls Barstools Due to Fall Hazard; New Instructions Provided; Sold Exclusively at Bed Bath & Beyond
sampleRecalls.description: This recall involves LF Products Sawyer swivel barstools sold in black, white, distressed blue, and dark brown. The recalled barstools have a tan cushion and were sold in two heights, 24 inches and 30 inches. "LF Products" is printed on a label affixed to the barstool.
hazards: Screws on the barstools can loosen, posing a fall hazard to the user.
injuries: The firm has received 15 reports of loosened hardware resulting in four reports of fall injuries.
units: About 108,000 (in addition, about 6,200 units were sold in Canada)

sampleRecalls.title: Gap Recalls Baby Swimsuits Due to Strangulation Hazard
sampleRecalls.description: The baby swimsuits were sold in two styles: number 706260 is blue and white and number 700452 is red and white. The style number can be found on the label located on the swimsuit. Both swimsuits were sold in infant sizes up to 24 months and are made of a synthetic knit stretch fabric of polyester and spandex. The stra…
hazards: The swimsuits have halter straps that were manufactured too short causing the plastic ring located at the neck of the swimsuit to press against the child's thr…
injuries: The firm has received two consumer complaints. No injuries have been reported.
units: About 6,500 in the U.S. and about 480 in Canada

sampleRecalls.title: Gap Recalls Children’s Coats; Toggles Can Break and Detach Posing Choking Hazard
sampleRecalls.description: This recall involves three different children's coat styles with toggles including Rose Toggle Hoodie (style 600298), Pink Duffle Coat (style 600344), and Blue Plaid and Mojave Toggle Coat (style 600942). The coats were sold in infant sizes up to 24 months and are made of cotton, polyester, or polyester/acrylic. The t…
hazards: The coats have toggle fasteners that could break and detach from the coat, posing a choking hazard to young children.
units: About 19,200 in the U.S. and 3,900 in Canada
